stories.headline: [NZ Herald] Crude prices plunge, stocks surge as US and Iran agree ceasefire Oil prices plunged Wednesday while stocks rallied after the United States and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire that will involve Tehran temporarily
stories.summary: The United States and Iran have agreed to a two-week ceasefire, resulting in a significant drop in crude oil prices. West Texas Intermediate crude prices fell by almost 20%, while Brent crude prices decreased by as much as 16%. Futures for US crude oil sank 14.3% to $96.83 a barrel, and Brent crude oil dropped 13.3% to $94.74. The news led to a surge in stock markets, with the ASX rising 2.6% and adding $80 billion in value. Miners, banks, and travel stocks rallied, while energy stocks declined. The ceasefire agreement has eased tensions and led to a decrease in oil prices, boosting investor confidence and causing a shift in the market. The temporary ceasefire will see Tehran reopen certain activities, further impacting the global oil market.

stories.headline: [Al Jazeera] Netanyahu says US-Iran ceasefire ‘does not include Lebanon’ Israeli prime minister welcomes US decision to suspend attacks on Iran, but says the truce does not apply to Lebanon
stories.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu welcomed the US decision to suspend attacks on Iran for two weeks, but stated that the ceasefire does not apply to Lebanon. Netanyahu's office issued a statement supporting US President Donald Trump's decision, subject to Iran opening the Strait of Hormuz and stopping attacks on the US and regional countries. Israel also backs US efforts to ensure Iran no longer poses a nuclear or missile threat. However, Netanyahu emphasized that the two-week ceasefire "does not include Lebanon", implying that Israel reserves the right to take action against Hezbollah in Lebanon. The statement was issued in English, with no Hebrew version released, and comes after Trump's announcement of a temporary halt to strikes against Iran.

stories.headline: [WION] Netanyahu issues first response on Iran-US ceasefire but Lebanon NOT INCLUDED The US and Iran announced a two-week ceasefire after weeks of fighting, with Israel backing the pause despite not joining talks
stories.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has accepted a US-proposed ceasefire with Iran, following a two-week agreement between the US and Iran to pause fighting. The ceasefire does not apply to Lebanon, with Netanyahu warning that Israel will "respond forcefully" to any attacks from the country. The agreement came after weeks of fighting and hours after US President Donald Trump threatened to destroy Iran's "whole civilization." Iran initially rejected the truce, but later accepted it. The ceasefire aims to pause fighting for two weeks, with Israel backing the pause despite not being part of the talks. Netanyahu welcomed the US decision to suspend attacks on Iran, but emphasized that Israel's security remains a top priority. The situation remains uncertain, with much still to be clarified about the agreement.

stories.headline: [The Guardian] Trump says uranium will be ‘taken care of’ - as it happened This blog has now closed
stories.summary: President Donald Trump stated that Iran's uranium "will be perfectly taken care of" in a telephone interview with AFP following the ceasefire between the US and Iran. Trump claimed the provisional ceasefire as a "total and complete victory." The issue of uranium enrichment arose after Trump withdrew from a multilateral nuclear deal in 2018, which had limited Iran's uranium stockpile to less than 4% enriched. After the agreement fell apart, Iran began producing 60% highly enriched uranium (HEU). Trump's comment suggests that the US will address the issue of Iran's uranium stockpile, but did not provide further details on how it would be "taken care of." The statement comes as part of a broader discussion on the ceasefire and its implications for US-Iran relations.

stories.headline: iPhone Fold may debut with iPhone 18 Pro despite earlier delay concerns Bloomberg report counters earlier supply worries, says flagship device may debut alongside iPhone 18 Pro lineup.
stories.summary: Apple's first foldable iPhone is on track to debut in September alongside the iPhone 18 Pro and Pro Max, according to a report by Bloomberg's Mark Gurman. This contradicts earlier concerns of a delay due to engineering challenges reported by Nikkei Asia. The foldable device is expected to be introduced during Apple's September launch window, with Gurman noting that any constraints are not expected to impact the launch timeline. The report suggests that while availability might be limited, the device is likely to be unveiled on stage in September. Apple has not officially announced the plans, but sources indicate that the company is moving forward with the scheduled launch. The iPhone 18 Pro and Pro Max are also expected to be released during the same time frame.

stories.headline: CUPS Vulnerability Chain Enables Remote Attacker to Execute Malicious Code as Root User A critical vulnerability chain in the Common Unix Printing System (CUPS) that allows unauthenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary malicious code with r
stories.summary: A critical vulnerability chain in the Common Unix Printing System (CUPS) has been discovered, allowing unauthenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary malicious code with root user privileges. This vulnerability chain enables attackers to gain full root-level access to affected systems, potentially leading to severe security breaches. The vulnerabilities can be chained together to execute malicious code, giving attackers complete control over the system. The discovery of this vulnerability chain highlights the importance of updating and patching CUPS to prevent remote code execution and denial of service attacks. Affected systems are at risk of being compromised, and users are advised to take immediate action to secure their systems. The vulnerability has been identified as a critical issue, and users should prioritize updating their CUPS software to prevent potential attacks.

stories.headline: Google Maps rolls out AI-generated captions for shared photos Google Maps is enhancing user contributions with AI-powered captions generated by Gemini for photos and videos, simplifying the sharing process.
stories.summary: Google Maps is introducing AI-generated captions for shared photos and videos, powered by Gemini. This new feature aims to simplify the sharing process and boost user contributions. Gemini uses artificial intelligence to create captions for user-shared content, making it easier for users to add context to their photos and videos. The update also streamlines media uploads, enhancing the overall user experience. Additionally, Google Maps is rolling out other features to make it easier for users to contribute local knowledge, including a redesign of the Local Guides program. These updates aim to increase user engagement and improve the accuracy of local information on the platform. The new features are part of Google's efforts to enhance user contributions and make Google Maps a more comprehensive and user-friendly platform.

stories.headline: You Can Now Download Pokémon Champions On Switch There's also a free update for Switch 2 - Pokémon battles level-up today with the release of Pokémon Champions on the Switch eShop.
stories.summary: Pokémon Champions is now available on the Nintendo Switch and Nintendo Switch 2 as a free download. The game supports Pokémon HOME, a cloud service for Nintendo Switch and mobile devices, allowing players to team up with certain Pokémon from past games and Pokémon GO. A free update is available for Pokémon Champions on Nintendo Switch 2, which improves the game's graphics and requires an internet connection to download. Pokémon Champions is free-to-start, with no upfront purchase required to begin playing. The game's release is on April 8, 2026, and it is compatible with Pokémon HOME, enabling players to bring over Pokémon from other games and devices. An internet connection is necessary to download the update and access the game's features.

stories.headline: Giorgi Mamardashvili and Ibrahima Konate could see their Liverpool fates linked in Paris The Georgian goalkeeper steps into Alisson's shoes in the French capital, where Ibrahima Konate struggled last season
stories.summary: Liverpool's Ibrahima Konate is facing an uncertain future with only a few months remaining on his current contract. The French centre-back has been linked with a potential move to Real Madrid and is reportedly prepared to wait for an offer from the Spanish club. Konate's wage demands have been revealed, with the player seeking a significant pay rise. Meanwhile, Liverpool's defensive situation is under scrutiny, with the club being linked with defensive reinforcements. In a separate development, Georgian goalkeeper Giorgi Mamardashvili is set to play in Paris, where Konate struggled last season, stepping in for injured Alisson. Konate's contract situation continues to stall, and his future at Liverpool remains uncertain, with the player entering a critical phase of his career. Liverpool's defensive plans are likely to be impacted by Konate's decision.

stories.headline: Pies set for huge Friday night boost as 'tough' mature Craig McRae has cleared a star duo to return from injury in Friday night's clash against Fremantle in Adelaide
stories.summary: Collingwood coach Craig McRae has cleared stars Nick Daicos and Scott Pendlebury to return from injury for Friday night's clash against Fremantle at Adelaide Oval. The pair's return is a significant boost for the Magpies ahead of the Gather Round meeting. Collingwood has an eight-day break leading into the match, allowing the team to recover and prepare. Coach McRae has implemented a highly organized game style, utilizing handball to create scoring opportunities. The team's methodical approach by foot has proven effective in converting chances into goals. With Daicos and Pendlebury back in the lineup, Collingwood is expected to pose a strong challenge to Fremantle. The match will also see Angus Anderson make his AFL debut, adding to the team's excitement and momentum. The Magpies are looking to make a strong statement in their upcoming clash.

stories.headline: Mate talks revealed as Steven Gerrard issues transfer plea Today's Liverpool news sees club icon Steven Gerrard select his choice to replace Mo Salah while talks surrounding the Egyptian's departure have been divulged
stories.summary: Liverpool legend Steven Gerrard has identified Michael Olise as a potential replacement for Mohamed Salah, who is leaving the club after nine years. Gerrard believes the French star, currently playing for Bayern Munich, could fill Salah's boots, but admits it would be a hard deal to pull off. Meanwhile, Dominik Szoboszlai has revealed that he had private talks with Salah before the Egyptian's decision to leave Liverpool became public. Szoboszlai's conversation with Salah suggests that the winger's departure was discussed before it was officially announced. Gerrard has also mentioned Luis Diaz as another potential replacement, as Liverpool searches for options to fill the void left by Salah's departure. The club is exploring the market for suitable replacements, with Gerrard's suggestions being considered.

stories.headline: MLB players with strong starts for new teams in 2026 Getting off to a good start in the first 10 games with a new club doesn’t guarantee anything for the next 152, but it’s better than the alternative, right?
stories.summary: MLB players who changed teams in the 2026 offseason are off to strong starts with their new clubs. The first 10 games of the season have seen notable performances from players who signed with new teams during the winter. While a good start does not guarantee success over the remaining 152 games, it is a positive beginning. Top free agents from the 2025-26 offseason, including Kyle Schwarber and Kyle Tucker, are among those who have made an impact with their new teams. Other players, such as Conor Grammes, a highly-ranked third baseman, are also contributing to their teams' early success. These strong starts have set a positive tone for the season, but the players and their teams will look to maintain this level of performance over the long haul.

stories.headline: Slugfest over Mallikarjun Kharge's RSS remarks, Pawan Khera's claims on Assam CM's wife
stories.summary: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) has criticized Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge for his comments likening the BJP and RSS to a disease. The BJP has filed complaints with the police and Election Commission against Kharge. Additionally, a controversy has erupted over Congress leader Pawan Khera's claims about the wife of Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma. The Assam Police visited Khera's residence, prompting a protest by NSUI workers outside Assam Bhawan in Delhi. The BJP has slammed Khera's remarks, while the Congress has defended its leaders' statements. The exchange has led to a heated slugfest between the two parties, with each accusing the other of making derogatory comments. The matter is currently under investigation by the authorities.

stories.headline: Delhi to regularise 1,511 unauthorised colonies; no layout approval needed
stories.summary: Delhi will regularise 1,511 unauthorised colonies on an 'as-is, where-is' basis, benefiting around 4.5 million residents. The decision, announced by Union Housing and Urban Affairs Minister Manohar Lal, covers nearly 87% of the 1,731 identified unauthorised colonies in the city. The regularization process will begin from April 24, with no requirement for layout approval. Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal, not Rekha Gupta, is the chief minister of Delhi, and this move is expected to bring relief to the residents of these colonies. The Indian government has approved the plan, which aims to provide a sense of security and legitimacy to the residents of these unauthorised colonies. The regularization will be done without requiring any changes to the existing layout or infrastructure.

stories.headline: West Bengal Election 2026 Congress Manifesto Shifts To Cash Transfers, Women-Centric Welfare
stories.summary: The Indian National Congress has launched its welfare-centric manifesto for the 2026 West Bengal Assembly elections, positioning itself as a third option against the Trinamool Congress and Bharatiya Janata Party. The manifesto promises free education, monthly financial aid for women, and support for farmers. It shifts focus to direct welfare fund transfers, women-centric schemes, and healthcare expansion, with significant schemes like 'Durga Samman' for women's financial assistance. Congress President Mallikarjun Kharge unveiled the document, stating it reflects the vision and pace of development for the state. The manifesto's emphasis on cash transfers and social security marks a departure from the party's 2021 focus on industrial revival. The Trinamool Congress has also released its manifesto, promising enhanced healthcare access and increased financial aid for women, as it seeks a fourth consecutive term.

stories.headline: Kerala High Court caps Sabarimala footfall at 75,000 per day, mandates refundable security deposit
stories.summary: The Kerala High Court has capped the daily footfall at Sabarimala Temple to 75,000 pilgrims to enhance crowd management. As part of this decision, the Court has also mandated a refundable security deposit for pilgrims. The security deposit amount will be refunded upon arrival within the allotted time window or adjusted against prasadam of equivalent value. This measure aims to improve crowd control and management at the temple. The decision comes as part of a series of efforts to ensure a safe and orderly experience for devotees visiting the temple. The cap on daily footfall and security deposit mandate are intended to prevent overcrowding and promote a more organized pilgrimage experience at Sabarimala.

stories.headline: Global Market: Geopolitics overtakes inflation as top global risk for central banks
stories.summary: Global central banks now consider geopolitics the top risk to the global financial system, surpassing inflation. A recent survey found that almost 70% of banks ranked geopolitics as their primary concern, a significant increase from 35% in 2024. This shift in concern replaces last year's top worry of US trade protection. Central banks managing over $9.5 trillion in reserves are particularly concerned about the impact of geopolitical tensions on the global economy. The rising concerns over dollar dominance and strengthening gold demand are also notable. The war in Gaza and other global conflicts have contributed to the increased focus on geopolitics as a major risk factor for central banks, highlighting the growing uncertainty and instability in the global financial landscape. This change in risk assessment reflects the evolving global economic and political environment.

stories.headline: Central banks flag geopolitics as top global risk amid rising tensions: Survey
stories.summary: Central banks overseeing $9.5 trillion in reserves have identified rising geopolitical tensions as the top global risk, with 70% citing it as the primary concern. A survey of over 1,300 experts worldwide found that geopolitical fragmentation is being fueled by COVID-19, the war in Ukraine, and US-China relations. The report analyzes global risks through three timeframes to support decision-makers. Despite tensions in the Middle East, Gulf banks are holding up well due to solid financial positions and early regulatory action. However, the full impact on the sector has yet to emerge. The survey's findings are part of a larger report outlining five critical trends in geopolitical shifts in power, wealth, and trade, highlighting key risks for 2025. The report aims to support decision-makers in balancing current crises and longer-term priorities.

stories.headline: Trump Jr.'s Balkan Bluster: Diplomatic Nod or Political Stance?
stories.summary: Donald Trump Jr. visited Bosnia's Serb region, criticizing the European Union's role in regional politics and echoing sentiments expressed by JD Vance in Hungary. Trump Jr.'s comments, made at a panel discussion in Banja Luka, described the EU as "a catastrophe" or "a bit of a mess" that needed fixing. His trip, "Trump's Business Vision 2025," aims to expand the family business in South-East and Central Europe. Meetings are planned with right-wing politicians and business leaders in Hungary and Bulgaria, amid ongoing protests in Serbia and political instability in Bulgaria. The visit highlights a potential shift in US diplomatic policy and the Trump administration's interest in European geopolitics, particularly with regards to the rising influence of regional leaders like Dodik. Trump Jr.'s tour is seen as an opportunity for some Balkan politicians to score political points.

stories.headline: No fourth headline available, only three provided
stories.summary: INEC has dismissed calls for the removal of Amupitan, clarifying its actions against ADC leaders. The electoral commission's decision comes amidst ongoing political developments. Meanwhile, global news includes Iran's latest wave of attacks on the UAE, Bahrain, and Israel, in response to US-Israeli strikes on its steel plants. The attacks mark an escalation of tensions in the region. In other news, various media outlets are providing updates on national and international events, including sports, entertainment, and business. Vanguard, a Nigerian newspaper, is covering the latest news, breaking news, and analysis on politics, business, and investment strategies. The situation is being closely monitored, with investors seeking insightful news and analysis to inform their decisions. Overall, the global news landscape is dynamic, with multiple stories unfolding simultaneously.

stories.headline: Why stock market up today? Nifty50 above 23,900; BSE Sensex rallies over 2,800 points on US-Iran ceasefire
stories.summary: The Indian stock market surged on Wednesday, with the Nifty50 crossing 23,900 and the BSE Sensex rising over 2,800 points. The rally was driven by the two-week US-Iran ceasefire, a sharp drop in oil prices, and strong global cues. The benchmark BSE Sensex surged over 2,500 points, while the NSENifty50 jumped more than 3% to reclaim the crucial 23,900 mark. This reflects a sharp rebound in investor sentiment. The ceasefire and falling crude oil prices have boosted market momentum, with bank stocks also lifting the indices. The Sensex and Nifty had previously ended higher, with the Sensex climbing 787 points and the Nifty ending up by 255 points to 22,968. The market rally is driven by a combination of positive global and domestic factors.

stories.headline: Rs 15L cr added! Sensex zooms 2,800 pts, Nifty above 23,950. From Iran ceasefire to oil crash, check 5 key factors
stories.summary: Indian equities surged on April 8, with the Sensex and Nifty climbing over 3.5% each. The rally was driven by a temporary US-Iran ceasefire and a steep drop in crude oil prices, as well as positive global cues. The Sensex zoomed 2,800 points and the Nifty topped 23,950, adding Rs 15 lakh crore to investors' pockets. This marked the fifth consecutive session of gains for the Indian stock markets. The ceasefire agreement eased geopolitical tensions, contributing to the significant surge in the markets. The benchmark indices reached their highest levels since December 16, 2024, with the Sensex vaulting over 2,800 points and the Nifty surging past 23,950. The rally was also led by short covering, with Dalal Street experiencing a dramatic turnaround.

stories.headline: ₹12trn rally: Sensex up 2,600pts intraday, Nifty around 22,900; key reasons
stories.summary: The Indian stock market experienced a significant rally, with the Sensex rising 2,600 points intraday and the Nifty reaching around 22,900. The rally was triggered by easing geopolitical tensions, particularly the US-Iran ceasefire, which cooled oil prices and boosted the rupee. This development led to a global equity rally, with the Indian market being a major beneficiary. The India VIX slipped 19% to 20, indicating a decrease in near-term volatility. The Sensex and Nifty rebounded sharply, surging around 1.6% each, as improving sentiment and supportive global cues lifted investor confidence. The rally was also driven by hopes of an early end to the Iran-US-Israel conflict, which boosted investor confidence and led to a sharp increase in the stock market.

stories.headline: Sensex jumps 2,600 points: Why is the stock market rising today?
stories.summary: The Indian stock market has surged, with the Sensex jumping over 2,600 points and the Nifty rising 3%. This sharp rally is driven by a temporary easing of geopolitical tensions in West Asia, following a US-Iran ceasefire agreement. The ceasefire has calmed nerves across markets, leading to a significant increase in stock prices. Additionally, a steep drop in crude oil prices has also contributed to the market's upward trend. The surge comes after a significant decline in the market earlier, with the Sensex crashing nearly 1,600 points and the Nifty dropping about 2% due to rising oil prices. The market's recovery is a result of the improved global risk environment, with investors regaining confidence in the wake of the ceasefire agreement. The Sensex and Nifty have zoomed nearly 4% each, indicating a positive trend in the Indian stock market.

stories.headline: Asian benchmarks jump after oil prices sink in response to the Iran ceasefire
stories.summary: Iran and the US have agreed to a conditional two-week ceasefire deal, which includes the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz waterway. As a result, global oil prices have fallen sharply, with benchmark Brent crude dropping 13% to $94.80. The ceasefire agreement allows for passage through the strait under Iranian military management for the next two weeks. This development has led to a surge in Asian benchmarks, as global stocks had been volatile since the war began in late February. The price drop and ceasefire deal were announced after a deadline set by the US for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz. The agreement has brought some stability to the market, with Asian markets jumping in response to the decline in oil prices and the ceasefire agreement.

stories.headline: Fixing the Nuts-and-Bolts of the Economy Process reforms can improve the administration of India’s voluntary company liquidation pathways
stories.summary: India's economy, a developing mixed economy, is in need of "nuts-and-bolts" reforms to simplify and streamline operational processes, improving efficiency. These reforms involve small changes that can have a significant impact, such as improving the administration of voluntary company liquidation pathways. With a share of less than 2% in global exports, India must focus on manufacturing to scale and penetrating global markets. The country's large domestic market often leads to a mindset that products will be sold locally, hindering export growth. The Indian government has initiatives like the Digital India Programme, which includes the India Portal, to provide easy access to information and promote development. As the seventh-largest country by area and the second-most populous country, India's economic reforms can have a substantial effect on its 1.2 billion citizens and the global economy. Streamlining processes can help India increase its global export share.

stories.headline: Indonesia to Meet Steam After IGRS Rating Controversy
stories.summary: The Indonesian Ministry of Communication and Digital will meet with Steam representatives to discuss the implementation of the Indonesian Game Rating System (IGRS). The meeting follows the discovery that IGRS labels have been applied to games on Steam without government oversight, leading to inconsistencies with Indonesia's legal framework. The ministry aims to clarify the issue and ensure compliance with Indonesian standards. The review covers content production, evaluation, and distribution to ensure age classification compliance. The ministry emphasizes that IGRS on Steam is not an official rating and may be subject to administrative sanctions. The government will summon Steam for clarification and encourage them to check ratings on the official IGRS website. The meeting aims to protect users and ensure that game information aligns with regulations.

stories.headline: C2FO Claims Top IFC Award in Lisbon as Global Funding Surpasses Half
stories.summary: The International Finance Corporation (IFC) named C2FO the Best Supply Chain Trade Partner for 2026 at the 9th Global Trade Partners Meeting in Lisbon, Portugal. This award comes as C2FO surpasses a significant milestone of $500 billion in cumulative funding to businesses worldwide since its founding. The recognition highlights a global shift in corporate finance, with C2FO emerging as a leader in working capital solutions. The award was announced on April 3, 2026, and marks a major achievement for C2FO, which provides funding to businesses globally. The company's achievement of surpassing half a trillion dollars in funding underscores its growing influence in the global finance landscape. The IFC's award acknowledges C2FO's contributions to the development of innovative trade finance solutions.
